task_groups.title: Write tests: Open input validation, directory creation, and schema

subtasks.title: Write TestOpen_EmptyPath and TestOpen_PathIsDirectory
details: In internal/db/db_test.go, write TestOpen_EmptyPath: call Open(""), assert db == nil and err.Error() == "db: path must not be empty", assert no filesystem side-effects.
Write TestOpen_PathIsDirectory: create dir via t.TempDir(), call Open(dir), assert db == nil and error contains dir and 'is a directory, not a file'.
Write edge-case variant with trailing path separator to cover TS-02-E1.
Assert that for both invalid-input cases no new filesystem entries are created (stat the temp root before and after).

subtasks.title: Write TestOpen_CreatesDirectory and directory mode assertion
details: Write TestOpen_CreatesDirectory: construct path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "newsubdir", "apikit.db"); call Open(path); assert err == nil and directoryExists(filepath.Dir(path)).
On runtime.GOOS != "windows", assert os.Stat(filepath.Dir(path)).Mode().Perm() == 0700.
Write a sub-test for the 'parent already exists' case (TS-02-6): use t.TempDir() directly, Open into it, assert success.
Write TS-02-E2 variant: skip on windows or root; attempt Open("/root/no-permission/apikit.db"); assert err != nil and db == nil.

subtasks.title: Write TestOpen_CreatesSchema and TestOpen_Idempotent
details: Write TestOpen_CreatesSchema: Open a fresh path in t.TempDir(); for each of the six table names query sqlite_master and assert the name is returned; close db.

For each DDL-specific test (TS-02-19 through TS-02-24) add sub-assertions: read the 'sql' column from sqlite_master and verify key DDL tokens (UNIQUE, REFERENCES, ON DELETE CASCADE, PRIMARY KEY) are present.
Write TestOpen_Idempotent: Open same path twice; assert both return nil error; insert a sentinel admin_config row on first open; assert it is still readable on second open.
Write TS-02-E7 (interrupted tx recovery): Open a path, close it, Open again; assert all six tables exist.

subtasks.title: Write TestOpen_WALMode and TestOpen_CorruptFile
details: Write TestOpen_WALMode: Open a fresh db file; query PRAGMA journal_mode; assert mode == "wal"; close db.

Write a sub-test for TS-02-15/TS-02-56: assert that Open returns an error containing 'failed to enable WAL mode' for paths on non-WAL filesystems (verify error format via string check on a simulated path or document as logic-path verified).

Write TestOpen_CorruptFile (TS-02-57, TS-02-E5, TS-02-E6): write arbitrary bytes via os.WriteFile; call Open; assert db == nil, err contains file path, err contains 'failed to open database at', errors.Unwrap(err) != nil.
Write TestOpen_MemoryJournalMode (TS-02-16): call OpenMemory(); query PRAGMA journal_mode; assert mode == "memory"; assert no WAL error is returned.
Write TS-02-18: verify empty-path error does NOT contain 'failed to open database at'; verify corrupt-file error DOES contain it.

subtasks.title: Write TestOpen_ForeignKeys and TestPing
details: Write TestOpen_ForeignKeys: OpenMemory(); INSERT into api_keys with non-existent user_id; assert err != nil (FK violation).
Write ON DELETE CASCADE sub-test (TS-02-49): insert user, org, org_member; DELETE org; assert COUNT(*) FROM org_members WHERE org_id='org1' == 0.
Write TestPing: OpenMemory(); call db.Ping(context.Background()); assert err == nil.
Write TS-02-E8: OpenMemory(); create pre-cancelled context; call db.Ping(cancelledCtx); assert errors.Is(err, context.Canceled).

checks: All test functions compile without error: go test -c ./internal/db/...
Test functions reference correct table names, error message strings, and requirement IDs.
No implementation code is written yet; all test functions fail with 'undefined: Open' or similar linker errors confirming TDD order.
go vet ./internal/db/... reports no issues on test files.

task_groups.title: Write tests: WithTx, WrapError, sentinel errors, and timestamp helpers

subtasks.title: Write TestWithTx_Commit, TestWithTx_Rollback, and context/BeginTx edge cases
details: Write TestWithTx_Commit: OpenMemory(); call WithTx inserting into admin_config returning nil; query row after; assert value is visible.
Write TestWithTx_Rollback: OpenMemory(); call WithTx inserting then returning errors.New("fail"); assert errors.Is(err, retErr); assert row NOT visible.
Write TS-02-33: WithTx returns exact sentinel error from fn unchanged (errors.Is check).
Write TS-02-36/TS-02-E10: pre-cancel ctx; WithTx with fn returning ctx.Err(); assert errors.Is(err, context.Canceled); assert no rows persisted.
Write TS-02-E11: Close db first; call WithTx; assert err != nil and fn was never called (use a bool flag).
Write TS-02-35: WithTx with nil TxOptions (DEFERRED) succeeds; verify via successful commit.

subtasks.title: Write TestWrapError_Conflict, TestWrapError_Locked, and TestWrapError_Passthrough
details: Write TestWrapError_Conflict (TS-02-62): OpenMemory(); insert duplicate username; pass driver error to WrapError; assert errors.Is(result, ErrConflict).

Write TestWrapError_Locked (TS-02-63): construct synthetic SQLite error with SQLITE_BUSY code using the modernc.org/sqlite error type; pass to WrapError; assert errors.Is(result, ErrDatabaseLocked). No live database needed.
Write TestWrapError_Passthrough (TS-02-64): WrapError(errors.New("unknown")); assert errors.Is(result, originalErr).
Write TS-02-39: WrapError(sql.ErrNoRows) returns sql.ErrNoRows unchanged, NOT ErrNotFound.
Write TS-02-40: assert ErrNotFound.Error(), ErrConflict.Error(), ErrDatabaseLocked.Error() match exact strings.
Write TS-02-E12: wrap synthetic UNIQUE error via fmt.Errorf("%w", inner); WrapError returns ErrConflict.
Write TS-02-E15: FK violation on api_keys INSERT; WrapError maps to ErrConflict.

subtasks.title: Write TestFormatTime, TestParseTime, and timestamp edge cases
details: Write TS-02-41: assert TimeFormat == "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z".
Write TestFormatTime (TS-02-42, TS-02-65): FormatTime on a time with sub-second nanos and non-UTC zone; assert result matches expected UTC string; assert !strings.Contains(result, ".").
Write TS-02-E13: FormatTime(time in IST +05:30); assert result == "2026-07-17T09:00:00Z" and !strings.Contains(result, "+").
Write TestParseTime (TS-02-43): ParseTime("2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"); assert fields correct and Location == time.UTC; ParseTime("not-a-timestamp") returns non-nil error.
Write TS-02-E14: ParseTime("2026-07-17T14:30:00+05:30") returns non-nil error and zero time.
Write TestParseTime round-trip (TS-02-44, TS-02-66): t := time.Now() with sub-second; parsed, err := ParseTime(FormatTime(t)); assert err == nil and parsed.Equal(t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()).

subtasks.title: Write connection pool, OpenMemory isolation, and DB.Close tests
details: Write TS-02-45: Open a fresh db file; assert db.SqlDB.Stats().MaxOpenConnections == 1.
Write TS-02-46: OpenMemory(); assert MaxOpenConnections == 1 via Stats().
Write TS-02-47 isolation test: two OpenMemory calls; insert into db1.admin_config; query db2 for same key; assert empty result.
Write TS-02-29: two OpenMemory calls produce independent instances (same assertion as isolation); all six tables exist in each.
Write TS-02-30: OpenMemory(); db.Close(); subsequent db.SqlDB.Exec returns error.
Write TS-02-27: assert db.SqlDB != nil after OpenMemory or Open; assert no per-table methods (InsertUser, GetUser etc.) exist on DB type (static type check via compilation).

subtasks.title: Write property tests for initDB shared initialization and schema idempotency
details: Write TS-02-7: Open a file db and OpenMemory; call a helper verifyTablesExist(sqlDB) that checks all six tables via sqlite_master; assert both pass.
Write TS-02-8: after Open, db.SqlDB.Stats().MaxOpenConnections == 1.

subtasks.title: Write property tests for FormatTime/ParseTime, WrapError purity, and WithTx atomicity
details: Write TS-02-P3: iterate over a table of time.Time values spanning past/future, sub-second, non-UTC zones; for each, assert ParseTime(FormatTime(t)).Equal(t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()).

Write TS-02-P4: call WrapError with nil, synthetic UNIQUE err, synthetic BUSY err, unknown err, and a fmt.Errorf wrapped UNIQUE err; assert deterministic outputs each time; verify no global state mutation by calling twice.

subtasks.title: Write property tests for Open/OpenMemory invariants and isolation
details: Write TS-02-P6: call Open with valid path, empty path, directory path, and corrupt file path; assert: if err == nil then db != nil AND db.SqlDB != nil AND PRAGMA foreign_keys == 1 AND all six tables exist; if err != nil then db == nil.

Write TS-02-P8: call Open with empty string and with an existing directory; assert each returns non-nil error and nil *DB; assert temp root directory listing is unchanged.

subtasks.title: Write smoke tests exercising real execution paths
details: Write TS-02-SMOKE-1: Open with missing parent dir; assert dir created (mode 0700 on non-Windows), PRAGMA journal_mode == 'wal', PRAGMA foreign_keys == 1, all 6 tables present, MaxOpenConnections == 1.
Write TS-02-SMOKE-2: OpenMemory; assert PRAGMA journal_mode == 'memory', FK ON, 6 tables present, MaxOpenConnections == 1; second OpenMemory call is isolated.

task_groups.title: Implement sentinel errors, timestamp helpers, and DB struct

subtasks.title: Implement sentinel errors in errors.go
details: Create internal/db/errors.go in package db.
Declare: var ErrNotFound = errors.New("db: not found"); var ErrConflict = errors.New("db: conflict"); var ErrDatabaseLocked = errors.New("db: database locked").
Add stub for WrapError: func WrapError(err error) error { panic("not implemented") } — will be completed in task 4.2.

subtasks.title: Implement WrapError in errors.go
details: Import modernc.org/sqlite and use errors.As to extract the *sqlite.Error from the error chain.
Map error codes: sqlite.SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE and sqlite.SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY → ErrConflict; sqlite.SQLITE_BUSY and sqlite.SQLITE_LOCKED → ErrDatabaseLocked.
If err is nil return nil. If no SQLite error code matches, return err unchanged.
WrapError must be a pure function: no I/O, no side effects, no database connections opened.
Verify the exact constant names (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE etc.) against the installed modernc.org/sqlite version.

subtasks.title: Implement timestamp helpers in timestamp.go
details: Create internal/db/timestamp.go in package db.
Declare const TimeFormat = "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z".
Implement FormatTime(t time.Time) string: return t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC().Format(TimeFormat).
Implement ParseTime(s string) (time.Time, error): return time.Parse(TimeFormat, s).
Ensure round-trip: ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) == t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC() for any t.

subtasks.title: Implement DB struct and Close/Ping methods in db.go
details: Create internal/db/db.go in package db.
Import _ "modernc.org/sqlite", "database/sql", "context".
Declare type DB struct { SqlDB *sql.DB }.
Implement func (db *DB) Close() error { return db.SqlDB.Close() }.
Implement func (db *DB) Ping(ctx context.Context) error { return db.SqlDB.PingContext(ctx) }.
Add stubs for Open, OpenMemory, WithTx, and initDB with panic("not implemented") — will be completed in group 5.

checks: go build ./internal/db/... succeeds with no compilation errors.
Sentinel error string tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -run TestWrapError_Passthrough -count=1
Timestamp helper tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -run TestFormatTime -count=1 && go test ./internal/db/... -run TestParseTime -count=1
go vet ./internal/db/... reports no issues.
Requirements 02-REQ-9.4, 02-REQ-10.1 through 02-REQ-10.4 acceptance criteria met.

task_groups.title: Implement schema DDL, initDB, Open, OpenMemory, and WithTx

subtasks.title: Implement schema DDL in schema.go
details: Create internal/db/schema.go in package db.
Define a package-level var (or const) schemaStatements []string containing all six CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements verbatim as specified in the PRD DDL.
Order must be: users, api_keys, pats, orgs, org_members, admin_config (FK dependency order).
Include all constraints: UNIQUE (provider, provider_id) on users; REFERENCES users(id) on api_keys and pats; ON DELETE CASCADE on org_members.org_id.

subtasks.title: Implement shared private initDB function
details: In db.go, implement func initDB(sqlDB *sql.DB, skipWAL bool) error.
Step 1: sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(1); sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(1).

Step 2 (if !skipWAL): execute 'PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL' via sqlDB.QueryRow and Scan the result into a string; if result != "wal", return fmt.Errorf("db: failed to enable WAL mode: journal_mode is %q", mode).
Step 3: sqlDB.Exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON"); return error on failure.
Step 4: begin a DEFERRED transaction via sqlDB.Begin(); iterate schemaStatements and exec each; commit on success, rollback on error.
Step 5: return nil on success.
OpenMemory will call initDB(sqlDB, true); Open will call initDB(sqlDB, false).

subtasks.title: Implement Open constructor with validation and directory creation
details: Implement func Open(path string) (*DB, error) in db.go.
Validation (before any filesystem access): if path == "" return errors.New("db: path must not be empty").
Validation: stat path; if err == nil and info.IsDir() return fmt.Errorf("db: path %q is a directory, not a file", path).
Directory creation: os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0700); return error on failure.
Open connection: sqlDB, err := sql.Open("sqlite", path); check err and return it.
Call initDB(sqlDB, false); if err != nil, sqlDB.Close() and return fmt.Errorf("db: failed to open database at %q: %w", path, err).
Return &DB{SqlDB: sqlDB}, nil on success. Note: input validation and dir creation errors are returned as-is (no path wrapper).

subtasks.title: Implement OpenMemory constructor
details: Implement func OpenMemory() (*DB, error) in db.go.
Open connection: sqlDB, err := sql.Open("sqlite", ":memory:"); return error on failure.
Call initDB(sqlDB, true) — skipWAL=true skips WAL PRAGMA and validation.
If initDB returns error, close sqlDB and return the error.
Return &DB{SqlDB: sqlDB}, nil on success.
SetMaxOpenConns(1) inside initDB guarantees each :memory: DSN maps to exactly one private in-memory database instance.

subtasks.title: Implement WithTx transaction helper
details: Implement func (db *DB) WithTx(ctx context.Context, fn func(tx *sql.Tx) error) error in db.go.
Begin: tx, err := db.SqlDB.BeginTx(ctx, nil); if err != nil return err (fn is never called).
Call fnErr := fn(tx).
If fnErr != nil: tx.Rollback() (discard rollback error); return fnErr.
If fnErr == nil: return tx.Commit().
Do not wrap or transform errors returned by fn. WithTx does not install additional timeouts.

Core initialization tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -run 'TestOpen_CreatesSchema|TestOpen_Idempotent|TestOpen_WALMode|TestOpen_ForeignKeys|TestPing' -count=1 -v
WithTx tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -run 'TestWithTx' -count=1 -v
No panic("not implemented") stubs remain in db.go, schema.go, errors.go, or timestamp.go.
go vet ./internal/db/... reports no issues.
Requirements 02-REQ-3 through 02-REQ-8 acceptance criteria met.

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration and stub audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify call chains are complete
details: Verify 02-PATH-1: Open → validates path → os.MkdirAll → sql.Open → initDB(sqlDB, false) → SetMaxOpenConns → SetMaxIdleConns → PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL (QueryRow+Scan+validate) → PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON → schema tx → return *DB. Confirm each step calls the next function with no gaps.
Verify 02-PATH-2: OpenMemory → sql.Open(':memory:') → initDB(sqlDB, true) → SetMaxOpenConns → SetMaxIdleConns → (WAL skipped) → PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON → schema tx → return *DB.
Verify 02-PATH-3: Open(corrupt) → validation passes → sql.Open succeeds → initDB fails → sqlDB.Close() called → error wrapped with path → returned to caller.
Confirm initDB is called from exactly two sites: Open and OpenMemory; no third call site exists.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation and error wrapping correctness
details: Confirm that initDB error from Open is wrapped via fmt.Errorf("db: failed to open database at %q: %w", path, err) — check that %w is used (not %v) so errors.Unwrap works.
Confirm input validation errors (empty path, is-directory) are returned as-is without the path wrapper.
Confirm WithTx returns the exact error from fn without wrapping (errors.Is check in tests).
Confirm rollback error in WithTx is discarded (no double-error return).
Confirm WAL mode error string matches exactly: fmt.Errorf("db: failed to enable WAL mode: journal_mode is %q", mode).

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run go test ./internal/db/... -run 'SMOKE' -count=1 -v and confirm all five smoke tests pass.
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-1 asserts: dir created, mode 0700 (non-Windows), WAL mode, FK ON, 6 tables, MaxOpenConnections==1.
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-2 asserts: journal_mode=='memory', FK ON, 6 tables, MaxOpenConnections==1, two OpenMemory instances are isolated.
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-3 asserts: corrupt file returns (nil, error) with path in message and unwrappable driver error.
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-4 and TS-02-SMOKE-5 assert commit visibility and rollback invisibility respectively.

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit
details: Run: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")' internal/db/*.go — expect zero matches.
Run: grep -rn '// TODO' internal/db/*.go — review any remaining TODOs; none should block functionality.
Run: grep -rn 'os.Exit\|log.Fatal' internal/db/*.go — expect zero matches.
Inspect db.go for any bare 'return nil' in non-trivial function bodies that should return an error (e.g. initDB returning nil prematurely).

Confirm no unexported functions are exported by accident: go doc ./internal/db lists only Open, OpenMemory, WrapError, FormatTime, ParseTime, TimeFormat, ErrNotFound, ErrConflict, ErrDatabaseLocked, DB, DB.Close, DB.Ping, DB.WithTx.

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification and full regression run
details: Confirm that Open(path string) matches the signature expected by server_core (the caller per PRD Dependencies section): no context.Context argument, plain string path.
Confirm that OpenMemory() matches the signature expected by downstream test suites (handlers, auth packages).
Run go test ./... -count=1 to confirm no regressions in other packages that may import internal/db.
Run go build ./... to confirm the full project compiles after the db package implementation.
Document any gaps discovered: if initDB needs a context in the future, note it as a future enhancement rather than breaking the current API.
